reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 3 cfs @ Fort Collins , 3 cfs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir The warmer, spring-like weather this week has opened up a lot of the ice through the canyon. You will find plenty of fishable water as you go up the canyon. We've seen some small Blue Wings in the canyon! So make sure to have a few adult patterns if you are looking for our first consistent dry fly fishing of the year. Cold water temps are still keeping bug diversity and size down. Wherever you’re fishing around Fort Collins, expect small bugs. Think size 20–24 Midges and Baetis patterns. Try a Jujubee Midge or a Sparkle Wing RS2. A Charlie Craven’s Mole Fly or Shucking Midge can fool those trout that are sipping in the soft water. Spring is showing it's face around here, and we are starting to send out Guide trips onto the Poudre more frequently. Give us a call to get the low down! Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year.
